redcowBoundary: This tranche intentionally declares no REDCOW affordance tags. It contains no observed probabilities, audience claims, platform weights, performance statistics, or per-video assessments.

L0.label: local or already owned
L0.rule: Uses owned media, code-native graphics, local tools, and no required metered generation.

L1.label: licensed stock or free-account source
L1.rule: Requires asset-level license capture or a free stock/API account but no required metered generation.

L2.label: metered generation or specialist compute
L2.rule: Likely uses metered image, video, voice, or specialist rendering; verify price and terms at execution time.

L3.label: bespoke production
L3.rule: Requires a custom shoot, presenter, actor, location, or substantial manual production.

sources.notes: Direct raw checkout is authoritative; npm installations are not used for registry truth.
Asset-level provenance remains mandatory and identifiable people may not be placed in harmful, offensive, or implied-endorsement contexts.

archetypes.name: UGC or Avatar Product Proof
whenToUse: A real user, authorized presenter, or clearly disclosed synthetic avatar can demonstrate an actual product experience.
The product has an observable workflow or result that can appear beside the presenter.
whenNotToUse: No valid consent or likeness authority exists for the presenter or avatar.
The script invents personal experience, typical results, or product use that did not occur.
A material commercial connection cannot be disclosed clearly.

beatGrammar.purpose: Establish who is speaking and whether the presentation is authentic, sponsored, employee-made, or synthetic.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Show the authorized presenter or labeled avatar with a short context or disclosure label.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use the presenter's own voice or an authorized disclosed synthetic voice; do not impersonate a real customer.

beatGrammar.purpose: Name the exact product task or problem from a truthful perspective.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Keep the presenter visible or cut to the real starting product state.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Do not use fabricated first-person experience or unsupported superlatives.

beatGrammar.purpose: Demonstrate the real action and observable output.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use owned product capture, matched before/after, or a clearly labeled prototype beside the presenter.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration follows the visible product behavior and states meaningful limits.

beatGrammar.purpose: Clarify who the product is for, what result is not promised, and any material connection.
beatGrammar.visualRule: End on the actual output plus a legible disclosure or fit card.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Disclose sponsorship, employment, affiliate, gifting, or synthetic presentation plainly when applicable.

inputs.sourceRule: Human presenter footage requires identity, recording, editing, and intended-use consent.
Synthetic avatar footage requires provider lineage, authorized likeness and voice inputs, and an explicit disclosure posture.
Use an owned safe demo account and show actual behavior; label prototypes or simulated states.
The script must reflect genuine experience or transparent presenter copy, not a fabricated testimonial.

rights.transformRule: Preserve the speaker's real experience, disclose material connections and synthetic presentation, and bind every product result to observable capture.
hardStops: Synthetic presenter could reasonably be mistaken for a real customer

audio.mixRule: Authorized presenter or disclosed synthetic narration leads; product SFX must come from the real capture or remain clearly illustrative.

implementation.nextGap: Add a presenter-consent and material-connection manifest plus an avatar disclosure review fixture.

layers.content: Authorized human presenter or clearly disclosed synthetic avatar
Real product capture or labeled prototype
Material-connection or synthetic-presenter disclosure when applicable
Presenter captions

qualityRisks: Presenter and product panels are both too small
Generic testimonial language replaces product proof

archetypes.name: Comment Reply Short
whenToUse: A real permissioned or safely paraphrased question creates a precise answer, correction, or demonstration.
The reply adds enough original evidence or instruction to stand alone without the original thread.
whenNotToUse: The comment is private, identifying, hostile, or shown mainly to trigger a pile-on.
The answer merely repeats the comment or depends on copied platform UI.
The source comment cannot be quoted, paraphrased, or displayed safely.

beatGrammar.purpose: Present the minimum question needed for context.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a generic reconstructed comment card with a pseudonym or no identity unless attribution is required and permitted.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Read or paraphrase only the relevant question.

beatGrammar.purpose: Answer immediately in one sentence.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Transition to owned presenter, screen proof, or code-native answer card.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Do not bait, ridicule, or mischaracterize the commenter.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the mechanism, example, or exact demonstration.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use one proof carrier and keep the comment card offscreen.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Distinguish fact, opinion, and uncertainty.

beatGrammar.purpose: Make the answer useful beyond the original exchange.
beatGrammar.visualRule: End with an original summary card rather than the source platform chrome.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Invite further questions only if no engagement automation is implied.

inputs.sourceRule: Use only the minimum permissioned, attributable, or safely paraphrased comment text; redact identity by default.
Build a generic comment card with original typography and tokens; do not copy Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, or Reddit trade dress.
The reply must be original and independently useful.

rights.transformRule: Minimize or paraphrase the source question, remove unnecessary identity, add substantial original answer content, and use generic code-native UI.
hardStops: Unnecessary username, avatar, or identifying detail
Answer depends on engagement automation or an account action

audio.mixRule: Narration owns the answer; use at most one restrained UI cue for the question card.

implementation.nextGap: Build a generic comment-card component and a privacy/quotation review skill before claiming runtime support.

layers.content: Generic reconstructed question with identity removed by default
Owned answer footage, screen proof, or diagram
Original answer captions

qualityRisks: Reply becomes a callout rather than an answer
Comment card remains onscreen too long

archetypes.name: Document to Gameplay Explainer
whenToUse: A rights-cleared document, article, paper, or owner-authored text contains a sequence that benefits from continuous secondary motion.
The gameplay is explicitly a background treatment while document meaning remains primary.
whenNotToUse: The document cannot be quoted, summarized, or transformed under a verified rights posture.
The document itself contains diagrams, evidence, or layout that must stay visible.
Gameplay would trivialize sensitive, tragic, medical, legal, or otherwise high-stakes material.

beatGrammar.purpose: Identify the document and the exact question being answered.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a brief generic source card over owned, licensed, or procedural gameplay.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Attribute the source and distinguish quotation from summary.

beatGrammar.purpose: Convert the document into a clear causal or chronological explanation.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Remove the source card; use captions and occasional code-native labels over uninterrupted gameplay.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Paraphrase by default and quote only the minimum necessary text.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show one exact excerpt, number, or diagram when paraphrase alone would be ambiguous.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Pause or dim gameplay behind a legible excerpt card with attribution.
beatGrammar.audioRule: State the limits and context of the excerpt.

beatGrammar.purpose: Land the practical meaning without overstating the source.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Return to gameplay with one original takeaway caption.
beatGrammar.audioRule: End with the supported conclusion and uncertainty.

inputs.sourceRule: Record author, title, URL or local authority, license or quotation review, retrieval date, excerpt boundaries, and transformation.
Gameplay must be owned or explicitly licensed; public creator uploads are not stock.
Prefer original procedural gameplay when external gameplay rights are unclear.
Source and excerpt cards must be original and generic.

rights.transformRule: Paraphrase by default, quote only what the explanation requires, preserve attribution and context, and clear gameplay independently.
hardStops: Substantial copied text replaces original explanation

audio.mixRule: Narration carries the document meaning; gameplay audio stays muted or low and never competes with attribution.

implementation.nextGap: Add a document-excerpt manifest and a source-card renderer that records quotation versus paraphrase at beat level.

layers.content: Owned, licensed, or procedural gameplay as secondary motion
Brief attributed source or excerpt card
Original explanation captions

archetypes.name: Before and After Transformation Proof
whenToUse: The same object, workflow, screen, or measurable state can be shown before and after a real intervention.
The comparison can be captured with matched framing, definition, and conditions.
whenNotToUse: Before and after states use different definitions, conditions, crops, lighting, scales, or time windows.
The transformation is simulated, cherry-picked, or cannot be reproduced.
The subject is a person's health, body, finances, or other sensitive outcome without appropriate substantiation and consent.

beatGrammar.purpose: Establish the exact starting state and comparison conditions.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Show the before state with date, definition, or condition label and a locked comparison frame.
beatGrammar.audioRule: State the baseline without exaggeration.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show what changed between states.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use owned process footage, screen steps, or a compact method card.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Name the intervention and meaningful confounders or limits.

beatGrammar.purpose: Reveal the result under the same comparison definition.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use matched crop, scale, lighting, and metric display; avoid cosmetic transition tricks.
beatGrammar.audioRule: State the observed result only.

beatGrammar.purpose: Explain how the result was checked and what it does not prove.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a verification card or return to the split comparison.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Separate the observed change from causal inference or typical-results claims.

inputs.sourceRule: Before and after stills must be owned or licensed, identically defined, and bound to capture conditions.
Process footage requires ownership and participant consent.
Product or workflow results must come from a safe real capture rather than a mock result.
Use original labels, metric cards, and matched-state guides.

rights.transformRule: Keep before and after definitions and framing matched, disclose meaningful conditions, and never visually enhance one state to manufacture the effect.
hardStops: Mismatched crop, scale, lighting, definition, or time window
Synthetic or retouched result presented as observed

audio.mixRule: Narration states baseline, intervention, result, and limitation; avoid impact SFX that exaggerate the reveal.

implementation.nextGap: Add a matched-state comparison manifest and a review that rejects inconsistent crop, scale, lighting, and metric definitions.

layers.content: Owned matched before state with condition label
Owned matched after state with the same definition
Intervention, window, and metric definition
Verification and limitation captions

archetypes.name: Search Intent Answer
whenToUse: A stable, specific question can be answered directly with an owned explanation and evidence.
The answer can satisfy informational intent without pretending to know platform search volume or ranking behavior.
whenNotToUse: The query is invented, ambiguous, or used as clickbait rather than answered.
The topic is time-sensitive and the source set is stale.
The answer needs individualized medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.

beatGrammar.purpose: State the real question in plain language.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use an original search-style card with no copied engine trade dress.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Read the question once and begin the answer immediately.

beatGrammar.purpose: Give the concise answer before details.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Replace the question card with one answer card or relevant screen proof.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use appropriate uncertainty and freshness language.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the steps, comparison, or evidence behind the answer.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use motion cards, product screen, owned footage, or licensed stock only when each adds meaning.
beatGrammar.audioRule: One claim per visible evidence beat.

beatGrammar.purpose: State when the answer applies and the next safe action.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a concise summary card with source freshness or limitation cue.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Do not promise ranking, volume, or universal outcomes.

inputs.sourceRule: The query and answer must be editorially selected and supported; no platform analytics or keyword-provider read is implied.
Use a generic original query card with no copied search-engine branding.
Original steps, comparisons, and source labels carry the answer.
Use only as literal context with asset-level provenance.

rights.transformRule: Use original query and answer composition, cite current authoritative evidence where needed, and clear every optional stock asset separately.
hardStops: Fabricated search demand or ranking claim
Stale source on a time-sensitive answer

audio.mixRule: Narration delivers the answer; UI SFX are optional and never simulate a real search interaction.

implementation.nextGap: Add a query-answer brief that requires source freshness and rejects unsupported platform-demand claims.

layers.content: Generic original question card
Answer, steps, comparison, or screen-proof panel
Direct answer captions

archetypes.name: Review-Mined Product Proof
whenToUse: Genuine permissioned reviews reveal a specific recurring problem, objection, or use case that the product can demonstrate honestly.
The review excerpt can be paired with real product behavior and transparent material-context disclosure.
whenNotToUse: The review is copied from an unrelated site, cannot be verified as genuine, or lacks display permission.
Reviews were fabricated, selectively edited to reverse meaning, or incentivized on a required-positive basis.
The product cannot substantiate the result or typical-experience implication.

beatGrammar.purpose: Present the minimum genuine excerpt that names the customer problem or objection.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a generic review card with consented attribution or redacted identity and a source-context label.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Read or paraphrase the excerpt without changing sentiment.

beatGrammar.purpose: State how the product addresses the specific issue.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Replace the review card with a real product workflow or output.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Do not imply that one review represents every customer.

beatGrammar.purpose: Demonstrate the exact feature, result, or limitation.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use owned capture and factual labels; show a counter-limit when relevant.
beatGrammar.audioRule: State what was observed and what remains user-dependent.

beatGrammar.purpose: Disclose relevant commercial or incentive context and clarify product fit.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a concise original disclosure/fit card beside the real output.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Disclose material connections and avoid universal outcome claims.

inputs.sourceRule: Record review source, reviewer permission or applicable display right, purchase/use verification when available, incentive and relationship context, retrieval date, excerpt boundaries, and transformation.
Use a real owned product workflow or output that addresses the quoted issue.
Rebuild a generic original review card; do not copy platform stars, badges, avatars, or trade dress.
Product response, context, and limitations must be original and substantiated.

rights.transformRule: Use genuine reviews with permission or a reviewed quotation basis, preserve sentiment and context, disclose incentives or relationships, and pair every claim with real product proof.
hardStops: Review display permission or quotation basis missing
Review edited to reverse or distort meaning

audio.mixRule: Narration distinguishes the reviewer's experience from verified product behavior; avoid testimonial crowd or applause effects.

implementation.nextGap: Add a review-evidence manifest covering authenticity, display permission, incentives, relationship disclosure, excerpt integrity, and typical-results posture.

layers.content: Generic genuine-review excerpt with source context and consented or redacted attribution
Real product workflow or output addressing the review issue
Material-connection, incentive, or typical-results context when applicable
Product response and limitation captions

qualityRisks: Product proof is weaker than the testimonial
Generic platform review UI is copied
